pi_items.source: Task description confirms 'Collision Time: T+12 minutes (0.2 hours)'; calculation: 0.2 hours × 60 min/hour = 12 minutes
Task description: 'Own Ship: ... Lat=50.5 ...'; coordinate is valid (English Channel region, 50.5°N)
Task description: 'Own Ship: ... Long=-1.0 ...'; coordinate is valid (negative indicates west hemisphere, 1.0°W)
Task description: 'Own Ship: ... Speed=12.0 kts'; knots are standard maritime speed units (nautical miles per hour)
Task description: 'Own Ship: ... Heading=000 (North)'; confirmed by maritime convention where 0°/360° = North
Task description: 'Head-on: Container ship, Heading 180 (South), Speed 18.0 kts'
Task description: 'Head-on: Container ship ... Speed 18.0 kts'
Task description: 'Head-on: ... Heading 180 (South)'; maritime convention: 180° = South
Task description: 'Crossing: Ferry, Heading 270 (West), Speed 12.0 kts'
Task description: 'Crossing: ... Heading 270 (West)'; maritime convention: 270° = West
Task description: 'Overtaking: Tanker, Heading 000 (North), Speed 17.0 kts'
Task description: 'Overtaking: ... Heading 000 (North)'; maritime convention: 0° = North
Maritime near-miss detection standard: DCPA threshold < 0.1 NM is standard in ship collision avoidance systems for detecting close-quarters situations

pi_items.verified_value: Verified: 1 degree of latitude = 60 nautical miles (standard definition: 1 minute of arc = 1 nautical mile, 60 minutes per degree)
pi_items.source: Web search: nautical mile definition confirmed as 1/60 of a degree of latitude; flat-earth approximation matches task description
Verified calculation: Earth radius 6371 km ÷ 1.852 km/nm = 3440.065 nm (matches verifier.py line 35)

pi_items.verified_value: Bridge Command is open-source ship simulator from https://github.com/bridgecommand/bc
pi_items.source: GitHub repository confirmed: https://github.com/bridgecommand/bc; C++ simulator using Irrlicht 3D library; GPLv2 licensed

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims are verified and correct. The task defines a synchronized three-vessel collision scenario in Bridge Command (open-source ship simulator). Target collision time is 12 minutes (0.2 hours). Own Ship starts at 50.5°N, 1.0°W with heading 000° (North) at 12 knots. Three traffic vessels must be positioned to collide simultaneously: Head-on vessel (heading 180°/South, 18 knots), Crossing vessel (heading 270°/West, 12 knots), and Overtaking vessel (heading 0°/North, 17 knots). Collision detection uses 0.1 nautical mile threshold (standard maritime near-miss standard). All coordinates, speeds, and headings follow standard maritime navigation conventions (bearing 0°=North, 180°=South, etc.; knots=nautical miles per hour; nautical mile=1/60 degree of latitude).
